root_text.an5.7:1.2: Asitabyābhaṅgiṁ, bhikkhave, kulaputto ohāya agārasmā anagāriyaṁ pabbajito hoti, ‘saddhāpabbajito kulaputto’ti alaṁvacanāya.
root_text.an5.7:1.4: Labbhā, bhikkhave, yobbanena kāmā te ca kho yādisā vā tādisā vā.
root_text.an5.7:1.5: Ye ca, bhikkhave, hīnā kāmā ye ca majjhimā kāmā ye ca paṇītā kāmā, sabbe kāmā ‘kāmā’tveva saṅkhaṁ gacchanti.
root_text.an5.7:1.6: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, daharo kumāro mando uttānaseyyako dhātiyā pamādamanvāya kaṭṭhaṁ vā kaṭhalaṁ vā mukhe āhareyya.
root_text.an5.7:1.7: Tamenaṁ dhāti sīghaṁ sīghaṁ manasi kareyya;
root_text.an5.7:1.8: sīghaṁ sīghaṁ manasi karitvā sīghaṁ sīghaṁ āhareyya.
root_text.an5.7:1.9: No ce sakkuṇeyya sīghaṁ sīghaṁ āharituṁ, vāmena hatthena sīsaṁ pariggahetvā dakkhiṇena hatthena vaṅkaṅguliṁ karitvā salohitampi āhareyya.
root_text.an5.7:1.13: Karaṇīyañca kho etaṁ, bhikkhave, dhātiyā atthakāmāya hitesiniyā anukampikāya, anukampaṁ upādāya.
root_text.an5.7:1.14: Yato ca kho, bhikkhave, so kumāro vuddho hoti alaṁpañño, anapekkhā dāni, bhikkhave, dhāti tasmiṁ kumāre hoti:
root_text.an5.7:2.1: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, yāvakīvañca bhikkhuno saddhāya akataṁ hoti kusalesu dhammesu, hiriyā akataṁ hoti kusalesu dhammesu, ottappena akataṁ hoti kusalesu dhammesu, vīriyena akataṁ hoti kusalesu dhammesu, paññāya akataṁ hoti kusalesu dhammesu, anurakkhitabbo tāva me so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu hoti.
root_text.an5.7:2.2: Yato ca kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno saddhāya kataṁ hoti kusalesu dhammesu, hiriyā kataṁ hoti kusalesu dhammesu, ottappena kataṁ hoti kusalesu dhammesu, vīriyena kataṁ hoti kusalesu dhammesu, paññāya kataṁ hoti kusalesu dhammesu, anapekkho dānāhaṁ, bhikkhave, tasmiṁ bhikkhusmiṁ homi:

translation_text.an5.7:1.1: “Mendicants, sentient beings are mostly charmed by sensual pleasures.
translation_text.an5.7:1.2: When a gentleman has abandoned the scythe and flail and gone forth from the lay life to homelessness, they’re qualified to be called ‘a faithful renunciate from a good family’.
translation_text.an5.7:1.4: Because a youth can get sensual pleasures of this kind or that.
translation_text.an5.7:1.5: Now, all sensual pleasures are just reckoned as ‘sensual pleasures’, regardless of whether they’re inferior, average, or superior.
translation_text.an5.7:1.6: Suppose there was a little baby boy who, because of his nursemaid’s negligence, puts a stick or stone in his mouth.
translation_text.an5.7:1.7: The nursemaid would very quickly notice
translation_text.an5.7:1.8: and try to take it out.
translation_text.an5.7:1.9: If that didn’t work, she’d cradle his head with her left hand, and take it out using a hooked finger of her right hand, even if it drew blood.
translation_text.an5.7:1.11: I admit she’d know, ‘This will distress the child,
translation_text.an5.7:1.13: Still, it should be done by a nursemaid who wants what’s best for him, out of kindness and sympathy.
translation_text.an5.7:1.14: And when the boy has grown up and has enough sense, his nursemaid would not be concerned about him, thinking:
translation_text.an5.7:1.15: ‘The boy can look after himself. He won’t be negligent.’
translation_text.an5.7:2.1: In the same way, I still need to look after a mendicant who hasn’t finished developing faith, conscience, prudence, energy, and wisdom regarding skillful qualities.
translation_text.an5.7:2.2: But when a mendicant has finished developing faith, conscience, prudence, energy, and wisdom regarding skillful qualities, I need not be concerned, thinking:
translation_text.an5.7:2.3: ‘They can look after themselves. They won’t be negligent.’”

variant_text.an5.7:1.14: anapekkhā dāni → anapekkhā pana (bj, sya-all, km)

reference_text.an5.7:1.1: bj20.10, dr22.7, ms16A5_29, msdiv7, pts-vp-pli3.6, vri37.6
